architecture.description:
Three-layer stack: [Browser / Next.js Frontend] → calls → [Next.js BFF at /api/*] → calls → [Your Backend at BACKEND_API_URL]
The BFF is already built. It handles: - Cookie-based session management (httpOnly auth_user cookie) - Token forwarding (Bearer token passed through Authorization header) - Error normalisation and response envelope unwrapping
The frontend never calls your backend directly. All backend calls are server-to-server via the BFF.

var.name: BACKEND_API_URL
var.description: Your backend base URL. The BFF appends endpoint paths to this.

var.name: BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_PUBLIC_ID
var.description: The publicId of the operator's business account. Used as path param in all /operator/businesses/{id}/* calls. Set server-side only.

var.name: DEFAULT_OPERATOR_VENUE_PUBLIC_ID
var.description: If set, the admin dashboard uses this venue for stats and event listing before a venue is explicitly selected.

var.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_PUBLIC_ID
var.description: Same as BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_PUBLIC_ID but exposed to the browser. Needed only if client-side code must reference it.

response_envelope.description: ALL backend responses must follow this envelope. The BFF unwraps them before forwarding to the browser. If success=false, the BFF surfaces the error message.
response_envelope.note: The BFF also accepts a flat payload (no envelope) for backward compatibility, but the enveloped format is preferred and required for new endpoints.

operator_auth.description: Operators log in via POST /operator-auth/login. The BFF receives the sessionToken and: 1. Sets it as an httpOnly cookie (auth_user) containing the user object 2. Returns the token as accessToken in the response body 3. The frontend stores it in localStorage (authStore) for attaching to subsequent BFF calls as Authorization: Bearer {token}

authentication.session_check: The BFF checks two things on protected admin routes: 1. auth_user httpOnly cookie must exist and workspace === 'admin' 2. Authorization: Bearer {token} header must be present (for calls that proxy to your backend) Return 401 if the cookie is missing. The BFF handles this — your backend only needs the Bearer token.

endpoint.description: Main admin login. Called by the BFF when an operator submits credentials at /auth/login. Returns a session token valid for subsequent operator calls.
Sends a one-time password to the member's contact (phone or email). Used in the public-facing member login flow (not admin).
Verifies the OTP and returns a member session token.

Returns all published events for a venue. Called by: - The public-facing venue detail page - The admin panel's event listing (via the BFF /api/admin/events) Events with status OPEN or PUBLISHED are shown publicly.
endpoint.response: { "items": [ { "eventPublicId": "evt_abc", "title": "Special Night", "description": "...", // nullable "startAt": "2026-05-01T20:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-05-02T02:00:00Z", "status": "PUBLISHED", // DRAFT | PUBLISHED | OPEN | CANCELLED "reservationMode": "OPEN", "capacityTotal": 100, // nullable "reservedCount": 42, "requiresForm": false, "visibility": "PUBLIC", "customerCancellationAllowed": true, "customerCancellationCutoffMinutes": 60, // nullable "checkinOtpPolicy": "REQUIRED" } ] }

endpoint.description:
PUBLIC SEARCH ENDPOINT — This is the main discovery feature of DesireMap. The frontend search page calls this to list/filter venues (FKK clubs, Laufhäuser, studios). Currently not implemented; the search page shows empty results.
The frontend BFF route /api/establishments needs to proxy to this endpoint. NOTE: The BFF route /api/establishments does not exist yet either — both the backend endpoint AND the BFF route need to be created (backend first).

param.name: type
param.description: Establishment type: fkk_club | laufhaus | studio | other

endpoint.description: Single venue detail page. Called by /venue/{slug} and /bordell/{slug} routes. Currently the venue detail page uses mock data from a local file.

Returns the list of supported cities with their numeric IDs. IMPORTANT: The frontend has a static city list with slugs (berlin, hamburg, etc.) but the venue creation form needs a numeric cityId for the backend. This endpoint provides the mapping.
The frontend's 9 static cities are: berlin, hamburg, muenchen, koeln, frankfurt, duesseldorf, stuttgart, nuernberg, karlsruhe
Please ensure these slugs (or the display names) are matchable to the backend cityIds.

Returns available service type IDs. The venue creation form currently asks admins to type serviceTypeIds manually as comma-separated numbers. This endpoint allows replacing that with a proper dropdown.
Lists all operators belonging to the business account. Shown in the Admin Panel → Operators workspace.

param.name: businessPublicId
param.description: Comes from BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_PUBLIC_ID env var or the session's businessAccountPublicId

endpoint.description: Creates a new venue (Betrieb) under the business account. Called from Admin Panel → Venues workspace → "Neue Location anlegen" form.
endpoint.request: { "name": "Club Exklusiv", // required "addressLine": "Musterstraße 1, Berlin", // required "cityId": 1, // required — numeric ID from /public/cities "serviceTypeIds": [1, 3], // required — array of service type IDs "website": "https://...", // optional "publicEmail": "info@club.de", // optional "publicPhone": "+4930123456", // optional "generalNote": "Interne Notiz", // optional "reservationNote": "...", // optional "checkinNote": "...", // optional "policy": { // optional "maxActivePendingPerMember": 2, "maxActiveTotalPerMember": 5, "maxOverlappingActiveReservations": 10, "requireVerifiedPhoneForReservation": false, "requireVerifiedPhoneForCheckin": false, "pendingReviewTtlHours": 24, "lateCancelThresholdMinutes": 60, "note": "Stornierung bis 1h vorher möglich" } }

endpoint.description:
LIST all venues for the business. CURRENTLY MISSING. The Admin Panel → Venues workspace only shows one hardcoded venue (from the DEFAULT_OPERATOR_VENUE_PUBLIC_ID env var). This endpoint is needed to show the real venue list.
NOTE: The BFF /api/admin/venues route needs to be updated to call this once it exists. Please notify the frontend team when this is ready.
Creates a new event for a venue. Called from Admin Panel → Events workspace → "Event erstellen".
endpoint.request: { "title": "Themen-Nacht Mai", // required "startAt": "2026-05-01T20:00:00Z", // required, ISO 8601 "endAt": "2026-05-02T02:00:00Z", // required, ISO 8601 "description": "...", // optional "reservationMode": "OPEN", // optional: OPEN | FORM | NONE "requiresForm": false, // optional "capacityTotal": 100, // optional "generalNote": "Interne Notiz", // optional "visibility": "PUBLIC", // optional: PUBLIC | PRIVATE "customerCancellationAllowed": true, // optional "customerCancellationCutoffMinutes": 60, // optional "checkinOtpPolicy": "REQUIRED" // optional: REQUIRED | OPTIONAL | DISABLED }

endpoint.description: Returns full detail for a single event including lockVersion (needed for publish/cancel mutations).
endpoint.response: { "success": true, "data": { "eventPublicId": "evt_abc", "title": "Themen-Nacht", "status": "DRAFT", "lockVersion": 1, // IMPORTANT: required for publish and cancel "startAt": "2026-05-01T20:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-05-02T02:00:00Z", "capacityTotal": 100, "reservedCount": 0, "visibility": "PUBLIC" } }

endpoint.description: Publishes a DRAFT event. Requires expectedLockVersion for optimistic concurrency. The lockVersion comes from the event detail endpoint (OP-5).
Cancels a published event. Same payload as publish.
Disables an active operator account (temporary block).
Reactivates a disabled operator.
Permanently removes an operator from the business (cannot be undone).
Returns the logged-in member's profile. The BFF route /api/customer currently returns a stub from the cookie. Please notify frontend team of the actual path so the BFF can be updated.
Returns the member's visit history (checkins). BFF route /api/customer/visits currently returns empty array.
Returns gamification badges earned by the member. BFF route /api/customer/badges currently returns empty array.
Returns the member's active and past reservations. BFF route /api/customer/bookings expects this.

bff_routes.description: These routes live in the Next.js server at /api/*. They are the ONLY entry points the browser uses. The browser never calls your backend directly.

route.returns: { googleOAuth: false } (stub — Google OAuth not yet enabled)

route.status: STUB — returns only 1 configured venue until OP-3 is implemented

route.proxies_to: publish or cancel based on body.action
route.body: { action: 'publish'|'cancel', venuePublicId, eventPublicId, expectedLockVersion, reasonCode?, note? }

route.proxies_to: disable | reactivate | deprovision based on body.action

route.status: STUB — returns user from cookie only

city_mapping.description: The frontend uses these 9 city slugs throughout all URLs (/stadt/{slug}) and as display names. Venue creation requires a numeric cityId. Please confirm the mapping below or provide the correct IDs, then implement the /public/cities endpoint (PUB-4) so the frontend can fetch it dynamically.
city_mapping.action_needed: 1. Confirm or correct the display names match your city table 2. Fill in the expected_id values and reply to this document 3. Implement GET /public/cities (PUB-4) returning slug + id pairs

item.title: GET /public/establishments — Public search endpoint
item.impact: Main discovery feature, search page shows no results without this

item.title: GET /operator/businesses/{id}/venues — Venue list
item.impact: Admin venues workspace shows only 1 env-var configured venue

item.title: GET /public/establishments/{slug} — Venue detail
item.impact: Venue detail pages use mock data, not real backend data

item.title: GET /public/cities — City ID mapping
item.impact: Venue creation form requires manual cityId input; needs a dropdown

item.title: GET /public/service-types — Service type list
item.impact: Venue creation form requires manual serviceTypeIds; needs a dropdown

item.title: GET /member/reservations — Member reservations
item.impact: Reservation/booking flow in the frontend is fully built but returns empty

item.title: GET /member/profile — Member profile
item.impact: Customer dashboard shows data from cookie only, not real profile

open_questions.question: What is the exact path for the member profile endpoint? Frontend assumes /member/profile. Please confirm or correct.
Does the operator sessionToken also authorize member-facing calls, or is there a separate admin token hierarchy?

The /public/venues/{id}/events endpoint is used for both the public event listing AND the admin event listing. Should the admin use a different endpoint (e.g., /operator/.../events) that also returns DRAFT events? Currently draft events are not visible in the admin panel.
What are the valid roleCode values for operator reactivation? Frontend currently sends "VENUE_MANAGER" as default. Please provide the full list.
What are the valid checkinOtpPolicy values? Frontend currently sends "REQUIRED". Please confirm the full enum.

The /public/establishments search endpoint: should venue slugs be generated by the backend, or does the frontend send a slug on creation? The venue detail URL pattern is /bordell/{slug} or /venue/{slug}.
Token expiry: what is the default sessionToken TTL from /operator-auth/login? Should the frontend handle token refresh, or redirect to login on 401?
